name: Nederlandse Kooikerhondje
description: The Nederlandse Kooikerhondje is a Dutch breed that was traditionally used to lure ducks into traps called 'kooien' using its distinctive white-tipped tail. They are active, intelligent dogs with a striking orange and white coat and black-tipped ears that give them an elegant appearance. After nearly going extinct in the 1930s, careful breeding efforts restored the population and today they are cherished companion dogs in the Netherlands and beyond.

conditions_detail.name: Polymyositis
conditions_detail.description: Polymyositis is an immune-mediated inflammatory disease affecting skeletal muscles throughout the body, causing generalised weakness, muscle atrophy, and pain. It can be idiopathic or occur secondary to neoplasia or other immune-mediated diseases.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Immunosuppressive therapy with prednisolone is the mainstay. Azathioprine is added for steroid-sparing effect. Underlying neoplasia must be ruled out and treated. Prognosis is variable.
prevention_tips: Exclude underlying neoplasia with thorough work-up

conditions_detail.name: Epilepsy
conditions_detail.description: Epilepsy is the most common neurological disorder in dogs, characterised by recurrent seizures. Idiopathic (genetic) epilepsy is the most frequent form in young to middle-aged dogs with no underlying structural brain lesion.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Anticonvulsant drugs (phenobarbital, potassium bromide, levetiracetam) control seizures in the majority of cases. Treatment is lifelong. Cluster seizures and status epilepticus require emergency treatment.
prevention_tips: DNA testing available for several breeds
Avoid breeding affected dogs with idiopathic epilepsy
